title: Failure Design, Defer, Panic, Recover, and Resource Ownership · Language Lens: Defer Resource Ownership
summary: A multi-stage import must close owned resources, preserve partial-progress policy, and avoid converting programming defects into success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go defer resource ownership increment in a resource-safe import workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acquire and release resources in the owning scope, check close or flush failures when relevant, and avoid loop-defer retention.
Design error contracts that state operation, relevant identity, cause, retry meaning, and partial-result policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
